scope: Review-only validation of the execution governance chain before Dispatch Runtime and Worker Invocation Runtime implementation.

questions.can_every_stage_be_reconstructed_from_re: Yes for implemented stages through worker assignment. Dispatch and invocation are foundation-only and not runtime-reconstructable yet.
missing_references_between_stages: No blocking missing references from proposal to worker assignment
No canonical human_prompt_reference or router reference is required by Proposal Runtime
No single end-to-end execution chain id exists yet
questions.authority_leaks: No authority leaks identified in reviewed certified runtimes.
lifecycle_gaps: Completion and result persistence not implemented
Cancellation and expiry are not uniformly implemented downstream
duplicate_concepts: Multiple status fields exist but are boundary-specific
Risk is controlled if future runtimes preserve append-only artifacts instead of mutating upstream statuses
missing_fail_closed_boundaries: Dispatch and invocation fail-closed behavior is defined but not runtime-enforced
Execution, result, completion, and termination fail-closed boundaries remain future work
questions.human_prompt_to_worker_assignment_trace: Deterministic from proposal to worker assignment; human prompt to proposal requires surrounding router/orchestration evidence and lacks a canonical chain id.
remaining_architectural_gaps: Canonical end-to-end chain artifact or reconstruction report

certification_decision.reason: The chain is certified through worker assignment, but dispatch, invocation, execution, completion, and unified chain replay remain future work.

constitutional_invariant.worker_executes: not yet implemented in the reviewed chain
